text: How would you describe the current economic situation in Greece?
source: Pew Research Center Global Attitudes, Spring 2024 (Greece N=1,015)
notes: VERIFIED from raw data. Tests economic pessimism. Key signal for anti-establishment party voters and low-income personas.

text: How satisfied are you with the way democracy is working in Greece?
source: Pew Research Center Global Attitudes, Spring 2024 (Greece N=1,015)
notes: VERIFIED from raw data. Tests institutional trust dimension. Populist/far-right voters heavily dissatisfied across all countries.

text: Do you have a favorable or unfavorable view of Russia?
source: Pew Research Center Global Attitudes, Spring 2024 (Greece N=1,015)
notes: VERIFIED from raw data. Post-Ukraine invasion sentiment. Key test of nationalist/populist vs. mainstream party split. Hungary and Greece show notably higher Russia favorability than Western Europe.

text: Do you have a favorable or unfavorable view of the European Union?
source: Pew Research Center Global Attitudes, Spring 2024 (Greece N=1,015)
notes: VERIFIED from raw data. Tests Eurosceptic dimension. Far-right and populist parties (RN, AfD, Fidesz, Vox, PiS) drive unfavorable responses. High cross-country variation.

text: Do you have a favorable or unfavorable view of NATO?
source: Pew Research Center Global Attitudes, Spring 2024 (Greece N=1,015)
notes: VERIFIED from raw data. Security/defense dimension. Correlates with Russia view. UK and Netherlands show highest NATO favorability; Hungary and Greece lower.

text: Do you have a favorable or unfavorable view of China?
source: Pew Research Center Global Attitudes, Spring 2024 (Greece N=1,015)
notes: VERIFIED from raw data. Broadly unfavorable across all 9 countries. Tests whether trade-dependent personas (e.g. business-aligned) create a 'somewhat favorable' floor.

text: How much confidence do you have in U.S. President Donald Trump to do the right thing regarding world affairs?
source: Pew Research Center Global Attitudes, Spring 2024 (Greece N=1,015)
notes: VERIFIED from raw data. Majority no-confidence across all European countries. Populist/nationalist personas are the outlier — pro-Trump alignment is a defining signal for RN, AfD, Fidesz, Vox, PiS voters.

text: How important is religion in your life?
source: Pew Research Center Global Attitudes, Spring 2024 (Greece N=1,015)
notes: VERIFIED from raw data. Tests Moral Foundationalism dimension. Poland and Hungary most religious; Sweden, Netherlands, France most secular. Key calibration axis for MF dimension.

text: Do you think Greece's economic system needs to be reformed?
source: Pew Research Center Global Attitudes, Spring 2024 (Greece N=1,015)
notes: VERIFIED from raw data. Left and populist-right both demand reform but for different reasons. Tests interaction of Change Tolerance and Individualism dimensions.

text: How big a problem is the gap between the rich and the poor in Greece?
source: Pew Research Center Global Attitudes, Spring 2024 (Greece N=1,015)
notes: VERIFIED from raw data. Near-universal concern across all countries. Tests Individualism dimension: market-liberal personas (VVD, FDP-equivalent) create C/D floor.

text: Do you have a favorable or unfavorable opinion of New Democracy (ND)?
source: Pew Research Center Global Attitudes, Spring 2024 (Greece N=1,015)
notes: VERIFIED from raw data. Centre-right governing party. Tests mainstream conservative calibration. Older, higher-income personas most favorable.

text: Do you have a favorable or unfavorable opinion of SYRIZA?
source: Pew Research Center Global Attitudes, Spring 2024 (Greece N=1,015)
notes: VERIFIED from raw data. Radical left / former governing party. Tests left-wing populist calibration. Austerity memory drives strong feelings on both sides.

text: Do you have a favorable or unfavorable opinion of Greek Solution?
source: Pew Research Center Global Attitudes, Spring 2024 (Greece N=1,015)
notes: VERIFIED from raw data. Right-wing nationalist party. Tests nationalist/sovereignist dimension. Analogous to AfD/Vox in Greek context.

text: Do you have a favorable or unfavorable opinion of the Spartans?
source: Pew Research Center Global Attitudes, Spring 2024 (Greece N=1,015)
notes: VERIFIED from raw data. Far-right nationalist party. Tests extreme nationalist calibration. Strong unfavorability expected from mainstream personas.

text: Do you have a favorable or unfavorable opinion of the Communist Party of Greece (KKE)?
source: Pew Research Center Global Attitudes, Spring 2024 (Greece N=1,015)
notes: VERIFIED from raw data. Historic communist party — unique to Greek political landscape. Tests far-left ideological calibration distinct from SYRIZA.

text: Do you have a favorable or unfavorable view of the United States?
source: Pew Research Center Global Attitudes, Spring 2024 (Greece N=1,015)
notes: VERIFIED from raw data. Holdout question — never shown during calibration. Tests whether transatlantic sentiment generalises from WorldviewAnchor dimensions. Near-50/50 split in Germany; more favorable in UK.

text: Do you have a favorable or unfavorable view of the United Nations?
source: Pew Research Center Global Attitudes, Spring 2024 (Greece N=1,015)
notes: VERIFIED from raw data. Holdout question. Tests multilateral institution support dimension beyond EU/NATO. Generally favorable across Europe.

text: How much confidence do you have in Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy to do the right thing regarding world affairs?
source: Pew Research Center Global Attitudes, Spring 2024 (Greece N=1,015)
notes: VERIFIED from raw data. Holdout question. Tests geopolitics cluster coherence: Russia view + NATO view should partially predict Zelenskyy confidence. UK highest confidence; Hungary and Greece lowest.

text: How much confidence do you have in French President Emmanuel Macron to do the right thing regarding world affairs?
source: Pew Research Center Global Attitudes, Spring 2024 (Greece N=1,015)
notes: VERIFIED from raw data. Holdout question (calibration for France). Tests whether European leader confidence generalises from worldview dimensions. Confirmed boundary condition in Germany holdout (consistently underpredicted).

text: When children today in Greece grow up, do you think they will be better off or worse off financially than their parents?
source: Pew Research Center Global Attitudes, Spring 2024 (Greece N=1,015)
notes: VERIFIED from raw data. 3-option question (A/B/C only). Holdout question. Tests economic outlook dimension. High pessimism across Europe; France most pessimistic.
